design_evolution_notes.description: Figure 1 is a generic rectangular microstrip antenna reference used for context, not a separate optimized final design.
design_evolution_notes.label: Reference antenna illustration

design_evolution_notes.description: Figure 2 introduces the proposed rectangular microstrip patch antenna before final simulation.
design_evolution_notes.label: Proposed geometry

design_evolution_notes.description: The final patch is the simulated design reported with return loss, VSWR, input impedance, radiation pattern, and current distribution results at 2.25 GHz.
design_evolution_notes.label: Final simulation result

design_evolution_notes.description: The conclusion says the antenna can be easily fabricated, but the retrieved evidence does not include measured fabrication results or a distinct fabricated variant.
design_evolution_notes.label: Fabrication mentioned but not validated

identified_antennas: Figure 1 generic rectangular microstrip antenna reference
Figure 2 proposed rectangular microstrip patch antenna
Figure 3 final patch obtained after simulation
proposed_final_antenna_rationale: Figure 3 is the only geometry explicitly called the final patch, and it is the one tied to the reported return loss, VSWR, input impedance, and bandwidth results. Table I supplies the completed dimensions and feed coordinates that align with this final simulated design. The earlier figures and derivation subsections are best treated as background and design-synthesis steps rather than separate canonical antennas.
selected_design_rationale: The paper's canonical design is the figure explicitly labeled as the 'final patch obtained after simulation' and the associated parameter table. Figure 1 is only a generic introductory microstrip-antenna illustration, Figure 2 is the proposed geometry, and Figure 3 plus Table I carry the finalized dimensions and performance results. No competing antenna variants are developed or compared as alternate final candidates.
selected_design_summary: Single dominant design: a coaxial-probe-fed rectangular microstrip patch antenna on FR-4, simulated and finalized around 2.25 GHz with a finite rectangular ground plane.

unresolved_conflicts.description: Table I gives Length 31.43 mm and Width 40.57 mm, while the prose describes the patch as 40.1 mm × 31 mm. These appear to be the same rectangle expressed with rounding and/or swapped ordering, but the exact finalized geometry is not perfectly consistent across sources.
